source_jobs.title: Software Engineer Intern
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



What you’ll do

As a Software Engineer Intern at Simular, you’ll work closely with our founding team to help bring AI agents into the real world. Depending on your skills and interests, you may:

 - Contribute to core product development, building new features and improving performance.

 - Work with the infrastructure team on scaling systems and virtual machines.

 - Support Forward Deployed Engineering projects, writing scripts and integrations to adapt agents to customer needs.

 - Collaborate with designers and researchers to prototype new ideas and test them with users.

 - Gain hands-on experience in a fast-moving startup where every line of code can make an impact.



You might be a fit if

 - Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, or a related technical field.

 - Solid programming skills in Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, or a similar language.

 - Strong CS fundamentals and problem-solving ability; competitive programming background (e.g. ACM ICPC, IOI, Google Code Jam, or equivalent contests) is highly preferred.

 - Willingness to dive into different parts of the stack, from frontend to backend to deployment scripts.

 - Curiosity, adaptability, and eagerness to learn in an ambiguous, fast-paced environment.

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills.

 - Bonus: prior internship or project experience in AI, agents, or productivity tools.

source_jobs.title: People & Operations Generalist
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



We're looking for a People & Operations Generalist to help keep Simular running smoothly as we scale. You'll be the go-to person for our Palo Alto HQ - owning day-to-day operations, supporting our team through the full employee lifecycle, and keeping things organized behind the scenes.



What you'll do

 - Own office management: facilities, supplies, vendor relationships, and making sure the space works well for the team

 - Run payroll processing and coordinate with our external providers to make sure everyone gets paid accurately and on time

 - Manage onboarding and offboarding end-to-end: equipment setup, account access, first-day logistics, and exit processes

 - Maintain employee records, HR documentation, and compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions

 - Support benefits administration and coordinate with insurance and payroll vendors

 - Assist with recruiting coordination when needed: scheduling interviews, communicating with candidates, keeping pipelines organized

 - Identify and improve operational workflows — if something is manual and repetitive, you'll want to fix it



You might be a fit if

 - 2-4 years of experience in People Ops, HR, office management, or a similar generalist role

 - Bachelor's degree in Business, HR, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - You're organized, reliable, and comfortable owning things without a lot of hand-holding

 - You have experience with payroll tools and HRIS systems

 - You're good at juggling multiple priorities and keeping track of a lot of moving parts

 - You're based in or willing to relocate to the Bay Area



Bonus

Experience at an early-stage startup. Exposure to multi-jurisdiction employment compliance (US + international).

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Agent Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



At Simular, we’re building the next generation of computer user agents - AI systems that can actually use your computer for you. Our backend powers everything from live VM orchestration to agent planning and execution.

We’re looking for a generalist backend/infrastructure engineer who thrives in ambiguity, has strong architectural instincts, and wants to own big, evolving pieces of our system. This isn’t a narrow “DevOps” or “API engineer” role - you’ll be shaping how our platform scales and evolves over the next few years.

What you’ll do

 - Design and scale the systems behind Simular Cloud and our cross-platform agents.

 - Own critical backend services: APIs, data flows, billing, observability, deployment pipelines.

 - Tackle hard infra challenges: VM orchestration, reproducibility, parallel execution, reliability.

 - Spot when it’s time to split/refactor services and lead that evolution.

 - Explore new directions as the product grows - from microVMs to multi-agent orchestration, fine-tuned model endpoints, or community task galleries.

 - Collaborate with product and research to turn ideas into production systems.
   

You might be a fit if

 - You’re a strong programmer with solid fundamentals (Python, Go, Rust, or similar).

 - You have experience with some of: cloud infra, distributed systems, virtualization, CI/CD — but more importantly, you can pick up whatever’s needed.

 - You think in terms of architecture, not just code: you know when to hack and when to design for the long run.

 - You’re comfortable owning big ambiguous problems end-to-end.

 - Bonus: you’ve touched GPU scheduling, large-scale ML infra, or scaling SaaS systems.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, CUA Control
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



We're looking for an engineer to work on the control layer - the system that translates an AI model's intent into precise, reliable actions on a real computer. This means mouse movements, keyboard input, window management, UI element detection, and error recovery across macOS, Windows, and Linux.



What you'll do

 - Work on the low-level computer control stack: mouse/keyboard injection, screen capture, coordinate mapping, input simulation

 - Implement UI element detection using accessibility APIs (AXUIElement, UI Automation), DOM/a11y trees, and visual grounding

 - Help build the abstraction layer that lets our agent operate across OS platforms and application types

 - Tackle reliability problems: element targeting under UI changes, window occlusion, resolution scaling, cross-app focus management

 - Contribute to feedback loops: how does the agent know its action worked? How does it recover when something unexpected happens?

 - Work closely with the model and planning team on the interface between intent and execution



You might be a fit if

 - You've built OS-level input automation (CGEvent, SendInput, xdotool, or similar)

 - You understand accessibility frameworks - AXUIElement on macOS, UI Automation on Windows, AT-SPI on Linux

 - You've dealt with flaky element selectors, timing issues, resolution-dependent coordinates

 - You think carefully about reliability and edge cases

 - You've worked with tools like Playwright, Appium, PyAutoGUI, Hammerspoon, or similar



Bonus

Experience with screen reader internals, remote desktop protocols (RDP/VNC), game automation, LLM agent tool-use systems, or mobile device automation (iOS UIAutomation / XCTest, Android UIAutomator / Accessibility).

source_jobs.title: Dream Job Application - Didn’t See Your Role? Reach Out!
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



Who Are We Looking For?

Are you one of those awesome people who don’t fit into boxes—but know you’d make a real impact?

Maybe you’re creative, maybe you’re a problem-solver, maybe you just have a spark we don’t even know we need yet. Either way, if you’re excited about Simular and want to join us, this is your chance to make your own role!



What You’ll Do

 - Tell us who you are, what fires you up, and why you think Simular is a great match

 - Dream up your own ideal job, or show us how your unique skills could shake things up at Simular

 - Share your stories, your side projects, your passions—even the wild ideas you’ve always wanted to try in a great team



Why Apply?

 - We love meeting bold, creative, and curious humans!

 - There’s always room for new talents, perspectives, and fun personalities at Simular

 - If we don’t have a spot now, we’ll keep you in our Talent Pool and reach out when the perfect gig shows up



How to Apply

Hit ‘Apply’ and send us anything you think will wow us—your resume, a video intro, a portfolio, a poem, or just an awesome story about you.

We review every application with a curious mind and an open heart.



Simular is all about building, exploring, and having fun together. If you’re ready to make your mark—even in the most unexpected way—we can’t wait to meet you!

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



What you’ll do

 - Partner directly with early customers to deploy and adapt Simular’s AI agents in real-world environments.

 - Translate customer needs into product features, prototypes, and integrations.

 - Write scripts and workflows to adapt our agents to customer-specific environments.

 - Build custom solutions, tools, and workflows that showcase the power of Simular in production.

 - Act as the bridge between customer-facing teams and core engineering/research - turning feedback into roadmap priorities.

 - Be a “founding multiplier”: scrappy, resourceful, and deeply customer-obsessed.



You might be a fit if

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, or a related technical field.

 - Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, or JavaScript) and ability to quickly automate workflows.

 - Experience working with customers or in client-facing engineering roles. OR comfort working directly with customers and translating business needs into technical solutions.

 - Ability to thrive in ambiguous environments, balancing immediate problem-solving with long-term product thinking.

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills.

 - Bonus: experience with deploying SaaS or AI systems, consulting/solutions engineering, or customer-facing technical roles.

source_jobs.title: GTM Intern
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



What you’ll do



As a GTM Intern at Simular, you will work directly with the founding team to help define and execute our go-to-market strategy. You’ll get hands-on exposure to every part of GTM, including sales, marketing, partnerships, and customer success. Your responsibilities may include:

 - Researching and analyzing market segments, competitors, and customer needs.

 - Supporting early sales conversations and helping prepare pitches and collateral.

 - Assisting with marketing campaigns, content creation, and community-building initiatives.

 - Talking to users, collecting feedback, and synthesizing insights for the product team.

 - Helping design scalable playbooks for customer onboarding and success.

 - Rolling up your sleeves on ad-hoc projects that move the business forward.



You might be a fit if

 - Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Marketing, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field.

 - Flexibility to collaborate across time zones - some customer/partner calls may be outside standard working hours.

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills; comfortable talking to users and partners.

 - Analytical and research ability; you can structure messy problems and extract insights.

 - Highly organized, proactive, and eager to learn in a fast-moving startup environment.

 - Interest in technology, AI, and startups.

 - Bonus: prior internship or project experience in growth, marketing, consulting, or VC.

source_jobs.title: Growth Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for an engineer who cares about growth metrics as much as they care about code quality. You'll build the data infrastructure and product experiments that drive how we grow, working closely with the GTM team to turn user insights into shipped product.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and own the growth data stack: event tracking, attribution, experimentation infra.

 - Design and ship product experiments end-to-end (instrumentation → analysis → rollout).

 - Partner with GTM and product to identify growth opportunities and translate them into engineering work.

 - Improve activation, retention, and monetization through code and data, not just dashboards.

 - Build internal tools that let non-engineers run their own experiments and analyses.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of full-stack or backend engineering experience.

 - Strong SQL and comfort with analytics tooling (dbt, BigQuery/Snowflake, or similar).

 - Experience instrumenting product analytics (Mixpanel, Amplitude, Segment, or similar) is a strong plus.

 - Bias toward shipping; you ship measurable improvements, not just code.

 - Strong product intuition and ability to reason about user behavior.


BONUS

 - Background in PLG products or consumer apps.

 - Familiarity with mobile or web attribution frameworks.

 - Prior experience in a startup or high-growth environment.

source_jobs.title: Research Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



What you’ll do

As a Research Scientist at Simular, you will:

 - Shape the future of agentic AI by pioneering new research directions in planning, reinforcement learning, multimodal reasoning, grounding, human-agent. interaction, and alignment (e.g. reward modeling, automated task evaluation, AI safety).

 - Design and execute experiments end-to-end: from data collection and benchmarking, to model training and evaluation.

 - Develop new methodologies that push the boundaries of AI agent capabilities and reliabilities, advancing both academic and product impacts, in collaboration with world-class scientists and engineers.

 - Collaborate closely with engineers to integrate research prototypes into product

 - Publish and present results at top-tier conferences, contributing to the broader AI research community.

 - Advance the science hands-on through designing methods, building datasets, running experiments, and benchmarking against state of the art.





You might be a fit if

 - PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field, or equivalent research experience.

 - Experience owning the complete research lifestyle: leading and executing a research program from ideation all the way to publication.

 - Expertise in one or more of (but not limited to) the following areas:
   
   - Pre-training, post-training, or fine-tuning LLMs/VLMs
   
   - Reinforcement learning and/or LLM-based agents
   
   - Computer vision and multimodal learning
   
   - Representation learning and embeddings.

 - Consistent publication record as first author in leading conferences (NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, ACL, etc.).

 - Strong implementation skills in modern ML frameworks (PyTorch, JAX, etc.).

 - Demonstrated ability to run complex experiments, analyze results, and iterate quickly.

 - Passion for high-impact research with real-world applications.

 - Ambition and vision to redefine what’s possible in AI research and deployment.

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



At Simular, we're building the next generation of computer use agents — AI systems that operate computers exactly as humans do. As our first dedicated security hire in Singapore, you'll own security end-to-end: hardening the applications our users touch, the infrastructure our agents run on, and the new and evolving attack surface introduced by autonomous AI agents themselves.

 

This is a hands-on, builder-style role. You won't be writing policy documents from an ivory tower; you'll be reading code, fixing it, and shipping the tooling that keeps Simular safe as we scale.

 

What you'll do

 

• Own application security across our product surface: threat modeling, secure code review, SAST/DAST, dependency and supply-chain hygiene

• Harden our cloud and infrastructure: AWS/GCP configuration, Kubernetes, secrets management, network boundaries, CI/CD pipeline security

• Build the security foundation for our agent platform: sandboxing, permission boundaries, prompt-injection defenses, data exfiltration controls, and safe tool execution

• Partner with engineering and research to bake security into product design from day zero, not bolted on later

• Run incident response and lead investigations when things go wrong; build the playbooks so the next one is faster

• Drive vendor reviews, customer security questionnaires, and the compliance work needed as we move upmarket (SOC 2, ISO, etc.)

• Establish the security culture: lightweight processes, useful tooling, clear ownership; scrappy, not bureaucratic

 

You might be a fit if

 

• You have a BS/MS in Computer Science or equivalent experience, with 3-6 years in security engineering

• You're a strong engineer first: comfortable shipping code in Python, Go, or TypeScript, not just reviewing others'

• You have hands-on experience across two or more of: AppSec, cloud security (AWS/GCP), container/Kubernetes hardening, CI/CD security

• You understand modern threat models: supply-chain attacks, identity and secrets, web app vulnerabilities, infrastructure misconfiguration

• You're genuinely curious about AI and agent security: how prompt injection works, how to sandbox autonomous tool use, how to reason about data flow in agentic systems

• You thrive as the first security hire: you can prioritize ruthlessly, build from zero, and earn trust by shipping rather than blocking

• Bonus: red-team or offensive security background, experience securing ML/AI pipelines, or prior early-stage startup experience

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Agentic Applications
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



Our cross-platform desktop client is the main way users experience Simular’s AI agents. It looks like a chat interface at first, but behind it is symbolic code generation, debugging tools, execution panels, and more. We want this to feel as intuitive and elegant as the best productivity apps.

We’re looking for a frontend/application engineer with taste - someone who cares about design, simplicity, and user experience, not just pixels or code. You’ll be building the surfaces users live in every day, and what you ship may often be the design.

What you’ll do

 - Own and evolve our cross-platform desktop app and future client experiences.
   Design and implement intuitive, elegant UX for interacting with AI agents, editing code, and managing tasks.

 - Explore new interaction patterns that make powerful AI tools feel simple and natural.

 - Work across layers: from user-facing UI to system integrations with native APIs when needed.

 - Potentially extend to mobile or web apps in the future.
   Own your slice end-to-end: not just UI but also business logic, BFF APIs, even backend pieces when needed.
   

You might be a fit if

 - You can build beautiful, functional apps and you care deeply about UX.

 - You have experience with some of: desktop frameworks, web/mobile, or native APIs - but you’re not limited to one stack.

 - You’ve built things outside of work (personal projects, side apps) that show taste and ownership.

 - You’re excited by both design details and technical depth.

 - Bonus: portfolio of design-forward apps, experience bridging web/native, or prior work on developer tools/productivity software.

source_jobs.title: PhD Research Intern
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



What you’ll do



As a PhD intern, you will:

 - Collaborate with research scientists to advance methods in:
   
   - Planning and RL for computer use (e.g. behavioral cloning, RL on model weights, RAG-based domain knowledge)
   
   - Multimodal grounding (e.g. vision-only models, tree search, hybrid methods with large models)
   
   - Reward/judge modeling (e.g. error analysis, human evaluation, training judge models)
   
   - User intent understanding (e.g. modeling vague queries, preference learning)

 - Contribute to building datasets, running experiments, and benchmarking results

 - Explore novel approaches and help derisk Simular’s long-term technical roadmap

 - Document and communicate findings through internal reports or academic-style writing



You might be a fit if

 - Currently pursuing a PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field

 - Research background in at least one of: Reinforcement learning, Large language/vision-language models, Computer vision and multimodal perception, Representation learning

 - Experience conducting experiments and publishing or preparing papers in top-tier conferences (NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, CVPR, ACL, etc.)

 - Strong coding and prototyping skills in Python and ML frameworks (PyTorch/JAX)

 - Curiosity, initiative, and interest in bridging fundamental research with applied AI

source_jobs.title: People & Operations Intern
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



We're looking for a People & Operations Intern to help build how Simular works as a company; not just behind a desk, but out in the world. You'll support hiring, onboarding, IT compliance, office management, and employee experience, while also representing Simular at hackathons, career fairs, and community events.

This is a great role if you want to see how an early-stage AI company actually runs, from the inside out. This role requires a full-time commitment.



What you'll do

Hiring & Talent Engineering

• Support the full hiring pipeline: post roles, screen resumes, coordinate interviews, follow up with candidates

• Help with onboarding new hires: equipment, accounts, welcome materials, first-week logistics

• Represent Simular at career fairs, university events, and recruiting meetups



Events & Community

• Help organize and attend hackathons, tech meetups, and industry events

• Coordinate team events, offsites, and internal culture moments

• Be a face of Simular at external events: you'll meet candidates, partners, and community members in person



Operations & Compliance

• Help find and manage office spaces

• Assist with IT compliance: device management, security policies, access controls

• Coordinate with external vendors: insurance, IT providers, and other service partners

• Support employment compliance across multiple jurisdictions: contracts, visa applications, labor law requirements

• Maintain employee records and keep things organized



AI & Process Automation

• Identify repetitive ops workflows and help automate them using AI tools, no-code platforms, or scripts

• Build and maintain internal automations: onboarding checklists, compliance reminders, candidate pipeline tracking

• Develop your AI literacy by working daily with AI agents and LLMs as part of how we operate

• Help the team stay on the frontier: test new AI tools, share what works, and document best practices





You might be a fit if

• You're organized, detail-oriented, and can juggle multiple tasks without dropping balls

• You communicate clearly and professionally

• You're proactive; you see something that needs doing and you do it

• You're curious about AI and eager to build real AI literacy, not just use LLMs, but understand how AI tools can transform workflows

• You're comfortable with ambiguity and figuring things out on your own

• You're a people person who's energized by events and face-to-face interactions

• You care about building a great workplace, not just running one



Bonus

• Prior internship in HR, people ops, or startup environments

• Experience organizing events, clubs, or student organizations

source_jobs.title: AI Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



At Simular, we’re building autonomous agents that operate computers exactly as humans do: clicking, typing, and navigating across any app or workflow. While our research team builds the brain, we need someone to decide what the agent actually does. As our AI Product Manager, you’ll own the product roadmap end-to-end, choosing which workflows the agent masters first, defining how users interact with it, and driving the engineering team to ship at startup speed. This is a high-leverage, 0→1 role where your decisions directly shape what millions of users will experience.



What you’ll do



 - Own the product roadmap for Simular’s agent platform, prioritizing the highest-impact workflows based on user value, technical feasibility, and business opportunity

 - Write detailed, unambiguous specs that engineers can build from without back-and-forth. You set the standard for clarity

 - Run sprints for the Product engineering team: backlog management, unblocking, and driving on-time delivery

 - Conduct user testing, analyze usage data, and iterate rapidly on the agent’s interaction model and UX

 - Define success metrics for agent reliability, speed, and user satisfaction, then drive the team to hit them relentlessly

 - Keep research and engineering tightly aligned on goals, timelines, and trade-offs across time zones

 - Be the voice of the user internally, translating real-world pain points into product priorities with conviction



You might be a fit if



 - You have 3+ years of experience shipping complex software products. You can read API docs, discuss latency trade-offs, and hold your own in technical conversations

 - You thrive in ambiguity and love the chaos of early-stage building. You don’t wait for a playbook; you write it

 - Your specs are so clear they leave no room for misinterpretation. Engineers trust your documents

 - You have deep empathy for users and make decisions based on data, not assumptions

 - You’re a strong communicator who can align cross-functional, cross-timezone teams without friction

 - You have a bias for action. You’d rather ship and learn than plan and wait

 - Bonus: experience with AI/ML products, particularly in the agent or LLM space

source_jobs.title: Finance Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



We are building AI agents that work the way humans do - and as we scale across the US and Singapore, we need someone to own the financial operations that keep the company running. This is a hands-on role for someone who knows their way around multi-entity finance, tax, and the operational complexity that comes with a fast-moving startup.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Manage intercompany transactions between our US and Singapore entities, including transfer pricing documentation and compliance.

 - Own tax operations across both jurisdictions: Singapore GST registration and quarterly filing, US federal and state tax obligations, and coordination with external tax advisors.

 - Support month-end and year-end close processes for both entities, including reconciliations, journal entries, and preparation of management accounts.

 - Provide financial support for due diligence processes, including preparing financial data rooms, responding to investor queries, and coordinating with legal and auditors.

 - Contribute to internal FP&A and budgeting: building financial models, tracking actuals vs budget, and supporting CEO with financial planning.

 - Manage relationships with banks, payroll providers, and other finance vendors across both geographies.

 - Build and improve internal finance processes as the company scales.




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF

 - Bachelor's degree and above in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.

 - 4 to 6 years of experience in finance operations or accounting, with direct exposure to multi-entity structures.

 - Hands-on experience supporting a US entity - including familiarity with US GAAP, federal and state tax filings, and working with US-based advisors.

 - Experience with transfer pricing and intercompany accounting.

 - Familiar with Singapore tax requirements, including GST registration and filing.

 - Comfortable working across multiple currencies and jurisdictions.

 - Experience supporting financial due diligence for fundraising or M&A processes.

 - Strong attention to detail and comfortable working independently in a lean team.




BONUS

 - CPA, CA, or equivalent professional qualification.

 - Prior experience at a VC-backed startup.

 - Exposure to equity management or cap table administration.

source_jobs.title: Product & User Operations
source_jobs.description:
Where multiple locations are listed for this role, the position may be based in any of those locations, with priority determined according to the order of listing.



We're looking for someone to own the relationship between our product and our users, keeping them unblocked, turning their feedback into product insights, and building systems that make every interaction count.



What you'll do

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for user questions, feedback, and escalations.

 - Triage incoming user issues, troubleshoot, and partner with engineering and product to drive resolution.

 - Build and maintain support workflows, knowledge bases, and self-serve resources.

 - Aggregate and synthesize user feedback into product insights that influence the roadmap.

 - Identify opportunities to automate operational work using AI agents and internal tooling.

 - Track key user-experience metrics (response time, resolution rate, satisfaction) and continuously raise the bar.



What we're looking for

 - 2-4 years in product operations, user operations, customer success, or a similar role.

 - Bachelor's degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related field.

 - Experience setting up or managing customer support systems and ticketing platforms (e.g. Intercom, Zendesk, or similar).

 - Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to track metrics, identify trends, and turn user data into actionable insights.

 - Strong bias towards automation and building systems, not just resolving individual tickets.

 - Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to translate complex issues clearly.

 - Genuine interest in AI products and how they work.



Bonus

 - Experience with AI agents or workflow automation tools.

notes: Highly technical roles, strong focus on AI and engineering. Some non-tech roles, but engineering dominates.
